root_cause: Repeated DNS resolution failures are causing the container to crash and enter CrashLoopBackOff
evidence: Timeline shows 2 DNS-failure -> restart-backoff sequence(s) for container 'worker' within the recent incident window
Container 'worker' is now restartCount=6 with a current CrashLoopBackOff state driven by repeated startup failures
Representative DNS failure: DNS lookup failed for auth-api.internal.example: lookup auth-api.internal.example on 10.96.0.10:53: no such host
Representative BackOff: Back-off restarting failed container worker in pod auth-worker-6b49d8f9f7-nq2lm_default
DNS failures consistently target hostname 'auth-api.internal.example' before restart backoff begins
The most recent terminated state exited with code 1 before the current CrashLoopBackOff

causes.message: Container startup depends on resolving a required hostname before the process can stabilize
Repeated DNS resolution failures prevent the workload from completing startup successfully
Kubelet repeatedly restarts the container until it enters CrashLoopBackOff

pod:auth-worker-6b49d8f9f7-nq2lm: Pod is repeatedly restarting after DNS resolution failures
container:worker: DNS lookup failed for auth-api.internal.example: lookup auth-api.internal.example on 10.96.0.10:53: no such host
Back-off restarting failed container worker in pod auth-worker-6b49d8f9f7-nq2lm_default

likely_causes: CoreDNS or upstream DNS forwarding is unavailable or misconfigured
The application depends on a hostname that is missing from cluster or upstream DNS
Startup logic exits immediately when DNS resolution fails instead of retrying gracefully
suggested_checks: kubectl get pods -n kube-system | grep coredns
Verify the failing hostname from inside a debug pod and inspect the pod's resolv.conf
